adds.rationale: Tesla's Optimus humanoid program and FSD progression give the watchlist a robotics/autonomy beachhead the user's thesis explicitly names as a likely next wave; current watchlist has no pure robotics/autonomy single-stock name.

news_evidence.summary: Tesla Investor Day detailed Optimus humanoid robot development and Master Plan Part 3 covering the AI/robotics roadmap
Tesla Q4 2022 earnings call: Musk reiterated Optimus as a long-term value driver potentially larger than the auto business

removes.rationale: Clean-energy ETF is thematically adjacent but not aligned with the profile's named next-waves (rockets, robotics, biology, quantum, nuclear); freeing this slot lets us add a robotics name without breaching the 12-cap, and URA/CCJ already cover the nuclear sleeve.

news_evidence.summary: iShares Global Clean Energy ETF lagged broad markets in 2022 amid rate hikes and entered 2023 well below its 2021 peak; thematic fit to the profile's specific next-waves is weak
ICLN's top holdings are solar/wind utilities, not a clean expression of the user's nuclear or robotics theses; nuclear is already covered by URA and CCJ in the watchlist

rationale_overall: Watchlist is at the 12-cap with heavy AI coverage (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, SMH, partial BOTZ) and a nuclear sleeve (URA, CCJ), but zero pure-play robotics/autonomy single-stock exposure. Swap ICLN (clean-energy, off-thesis vs. the user's specific next-waves) for TSLA (Optimus humanoid + FSD as the most-investable robotics single name as of Q1-2023). Net effect: tightens alignment with the profile's named next-waves while preserving the AI core and nuclear sleeve.
